hero.title: I design UIs <highlight>the way devs</highlight> actually <highlight>want to build them.</highlight>
hero.description: Started as a frontend dev, switched to design — and never forgot how annoying bad specs are. I keep both sides in mind so your team ships faster with less friction.

caseStudyDetail.phase1Desc: Led UI/UX design for a leather goods manufacturer. Referenced Sapo POS and adapted it for simplicity. Designed 15+ core screens.
caseStudyDetail.phase2Desc: Client didn't proceed. Lecturer pivoted to SaaS. I reworked the UI and coded the income/expense module — fixing consistency across 3 frontend devs.
caseStudyDetail.phase3Desc: Full UI rebuild using v0.dev. Faster iteration, more polished output. Became the foundation for InvoiceAI.
caseStudyDetail.problemTitle:
Manual tracking.
No system.
No visibility.
caseStudyDetail.solutionTitle:
Reference, adapt,
then make it your own.
caseStudyDetail.reflectionText: Phase 1 was too safe — no AI, inconsistent colors, layouts that played it too careful. That's also why the rework happened. The real value wasn't the final product; it was learning how to lead design on a real project, work with developers, and course-correct across three restarts.

about.bio1: I'm a UI/UX Designer with a frontend development background — currently a 3rd-year student at Vien Dong College, graduating late 2025. Since my 2nd year, I've been involved in real-world projects through faculty connections, which means I've shipped work that actually lives in production.
about.bio2: Because I started as a dev, I design with code in mind. Every layout I create is component-ready, every spacing decision is intentional, and every interaction is technically feasible — so your team spends less time re-speccing and more time shipping.

discover.title: Discover
discover.description: Interview the client directly — to understand how they actually think about their product, not just collect a list of features.

research.title: Research
research.description: Study competitors and references — picking out what actually works, not just what looks good on Dribbble.

design.title: Design
design.description: Brief the client on direction first, then build out all screens consistently with handoff in mind from day one.

deliver.title: Deliver
deliver.description: Hand off polished design files or a working prototype — whichever gets your team shipping fastest.

experience.description: Joined the core team as Designer. Designed 2D marketing assets for 990TOEIC and created logos for both 990TOEIC and VNMentors.

footer.heading: You made it to the end.
footer.subtext: That means a lot. If something here caught your eye — or if you just want to say hi — I'd love to hear from you. I'm currently open to fresher/junior UI/UX roles.
